Implement the following plan:

# PRAGMA Web — Next Steps Plan

## Context

PRAGMA is a tech/AI agency website built on Next.js 16 + Turso, deployed on Vercel. Current capabilities: bilingual landing page (EN/ES) with 6 sections, and an admin panel for creating/managing client quotes (line-items + blueprint types) with PDF export and shareable links.

The goal is to improve **both** the public-facing presence (lead gen, polish) and internal operations (admin efficiency, notifications). Key pain points: no visibility when leads come in (contact form stores to DB silently), and the site still has placeholder content that makes it feel incomplete.

---

## Tier 1 — Quick Wins (high impact, low effort)

### 1. Email notifications for contact form submissions
**Why:** This is the #1 gap — leads submit the contact form and you have no idea until you check the DB manually.
- Install `resend` (Vercel Marketplace integration, free tier: 3k emails/month)
- Add a `sendNotification()` call in the existing `submitContactAction` server action (`src/features/landing/actions/index.ts`)
- Send a formatted email to your inbox with name, company, email, message
- Optional: auto-reply to the lead confirming receipt
- **Files:** `src/features/landing/actions/index.ts`, new `src/shared/lib/email.ts`

### 2. Admin dashboard for contact submissions
**Why:** Even with email, you need a place to review/manage all leads.
- Add a `/[locale]/admin/contacts` route showing all `contact_submissions`
- Simple table: name, company, email, message, date, read/unread status
- Add a `read` boolean column to `contact_submissions` table
- Nav link in admin sidebar/header
- **Files:** `src/shared/lib/db.ts` (schema), new `src/app/[locale]/admin/contacts/page.tsx`, `src/features/landing/lib/queries.ts`

### 3. Replace placeholder case studies
**Why:** Placeholder content signals "not real" to visitors. Even 2-3 real or realistic case studies make a huge difference.
- Update data in `src/features/landing/data/` with real PRAGMA projects
- If no completed projects yet, write aspirational but realistic case studies based on your service offerings
- **Files:** `src/features/landing/data/case-studies.ts` (or equivalent)

### 4. Set `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` on Vercel
**Why:** Already in TODO — sitemap and robots.txt use wrong domain without this.
- Set to `https://pragma-web-steel.vercel.app` (or custom domain when ready)
- Redeploy
- **Action:** Vercel dashboard or `vercel env add`

---

## Tier 2 — Medium Effort (solidifies the product)

### 5. Quote email notifications
**Why:** After creating a quote, you currently have to manually copy the link and send it. Automating this saves time on every quote.
- "Send to client" button on admin quote view that emails the shareable link
- Uses same Resend integration from #1
- Branded email template with quote summary + "View Quote" CTA button
- Update quote status to "sent" automatically
- Optional: notify admin when client accepts a quote
- **Files:** `src/features/quotes/actions/index.ts`, `src/features/quotes/components/QuoteTable.tsx`

### 6. Admin search, filter, and pagination
**Why:** As quotes grow, the flat list becomes unusable.
- Search by client name, company, or title
- Filter by status (draft/sent/accepted/rejected) and quote type
- Paginate (10-20 per page)
- Update `getAllQuotes()` to accept filter/pagination params
- **Files:** `src/features/quotes/lib/queries.ts`, `src/features/quotes/components/QuoteTable.tsx`, `src/app/[locale]/admin/page.tsx`

### 7. Quote templates
**Why:** You're probably sending similar quotes repeatedly. Templates eliminate re-typing.
- New `quote_templates` table (same fields as quotes, minus client info)
- Admin UI to save a quote as template / create quote from template
- Pre-populate the QuoteForm when creating from a template
- **Files:** `src/shared/lib/db.ts`, new template CRUD, `src/features/quotes/components/QuoteForm.tsx`

### 8. Analytics integration
**Why:** You need to know if anyone is visiting, where they come from, and which sections they engage with.
- Vercel Analytics (zero-config, built-in) for page views + Web Vitals
- Add `@vercel/analytics` and `@vercel/speed-insights` packages
- One-line integration in root layout
- **Files:** `src/app/layout.tsx`, `package.json`

### 9. Landing page polish
**Why:** Small UI improvements compound into a more professional feel.
- Add `next/image` for optimized image loading (currently using standard HTML)
- Review mobile responsiveness on actual devices (the responsive audit was done but things drift)
- Smooth scroll behavior for nav anchor links
- Loading states / skeleton screens for admin pages
- **Files:** Various landing components, `src/shared/components/layout/Navbar.tsx`

---

## Tier 3 — Larger Features (when the foundation is solid)

### 10. Blog / content section
**Why:** SEO-driven content marketing is the best long-term lead gen for an agency.
- MDX-based blog with `next-mdx-remote` or `contentlayer`
- Categories: AI insights, case studies, tutorials, industry analysis
- Each post bilingual (EN/ES) with proper i18n routing
- RSS feed, OG images per post
- **Files:** New `src/app/[locale]/blog/` routes, `src/features/blog/`

### 11. Client portal
**Why:** Lets clients log in to see their quotes, project status, and documents — professional and reduces back-and-forth.
- Client auth (separate from admin — consider Clerk via Vercel Marketplace)
- Client dashboard showing their quotes, statuses, and history
- Document sharing area
- **Files:** New auth system, new `src/app/[locale]/portal/` routes

### 12. Multi-user admin with roles
**Why:** When the team grows, you need more than one admin password.
- Upgrade from single bcrypt hash to a `users` table
- Role-based access: admin (full), editor (quotes only), viewer (read-only)
- Audit log of actions (who created/edited/deleted what)
- Consider Auth.js or Clerk for proper session management
- **Files:** `src/shared/lib/db.ts`, `src/features/auth/`

### 13. Distributed rate limiting
**Why:** Current in-memory rate limiter resets per serverless instance on Vercel.
- Replace with Upstash Redis (Vercel Marketplace, free tier available)
- Drop-in replacement for current rate-limit.ts
- **Files:** `src/shared/lib/rate-limit.ts`

### 14. Error monitoring
**Why:** You won't know about production errors unless users tell you.
- Sentry integration (Vercel Marketplace)
- Captures unhandled exceptions, slow transactions, and user feedback
- **Files:** `sentry.client.config.ts`, `sentry.server.config.ts`, `next.config.ts`

---

## Recommended execution order

```
Phase 1 (this week):    #1 Email notifications  →  #4 App URL  →  #3 Case studies
Phase 2 (next week):    #2 Contact dashboard  →  #8 Analytics  →  #9 UI polish
Phase 3 (week after):   #5 Quote emails  →  #6 Search/filter  →  #7 Templates
Phase 4 (ongoing):      #10 Blog  →  #11 Client portal  →  #12+ infrastructure
```

## Verification

After each tier:
- Run `npm run build` to verify no build errors
- Run `npm test` and `npm run test:e2e` for regressions
- Deploy preview on Vercel and test the flows end-to-end
- Check Vercel Analytics (once installed) for real-world performance


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/santiagocoronado/.claude/projects/-Users-santiagocoronado-PRAGMA-pragma-web/a25acb9e-7ab5-4d33-a5cc-4f4cd5e23222.jsonl

If this plan can be broken down into multiple independent tasks, consider using the TeamCreate tool to create a team and parallelize the work.

1	import { createClient, type Client } from "@libsql/client";
2	
3	let client: Client | null = null;
4	
5	export function getDb(): Client {
6	  if (!client) {
7	    const url = process.env.DATABASE_URL || "file:local.db";
8	    const authToken = process.env.DATABASE_AUTH_TOKEN || undefined;
9	
10	    client = createClient({ url, authToken });
11	  }
12	  return client;
13	}
14	
15	export async function initDb() {
16	  const db = getDb();
17	
18	  await db.execute(`
19	    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS quotes (
20	      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
21	      client_name TEXT NOT NULL,
22	      client_email TEXT NOT NULL,
23	      client_company TEXT DEFAULT '',
24	      title TEXT NOT NULL,
25	      description TEXT DEFAULT '',
26	      quote_type TEXT DEFAULT 'line-items',
27	      line_items TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
28	      currency TEXT DEFAULT 'MXN',
29	      discount REAL DEFAULT 0,
30	      notes TEXT DEFAULT '',
31	      status TEXT DEFAULT 'draft',
32	      valid_until TEXT DEFAULT '',
33	      locale TEXT DEFAULT 'en',
34	      problem TEXT,
35	      opportunity TEXT,
36	      deliverables TEXT,
37	      timeline TEXT,
38	      fixed_price REAL,
39	      list_price REAL,
40	      preferential_note TEXT,
41	      payment_terms TEXT,
42	      next_steps TEXT,
43	      scope_note TEXT,
44	      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
45	      updated_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
46	    )
47	  `);
48	
49	  // Migrations for existing installations (ignore errors if columns already exist)
50	  const migrations = [
51	    "ALTER TABLE quotes ADD COLUMN quote_type TEXT DEFAULT 'line-items'",
52	    "ALTER TABLE quotes ADD COLUMN problem TEXT",
53	    "ALTER TABLE quotes ADD COLUMN opportunity TEXT",
54	    "ALTER TABLE quotes ADD COLUMN deliverables TEXT",
55	    "ALTER TABLE quotes ADD COLUMN timeline TEXT",
56	    "ALTER TABLE quotes ADD COLUMN fixed_price REAL",
57	    "ALTER TABLE quotes ADD COLUMN list_price REAL",
58	    "ALTER TABLE quotes ADD COLUMN preferential_note TEXT",
59	    "ALTER TABLE quotes ADD COLUMN payment_terms TEXT",
60	    "ALTER TABLE quotes ADD COLUMN next_steps TEXT",
61	    "ALTER TABLE quotes ADD COLUMN scope_note TEXT",
62	  ];
63	
64	  for (const sql of migrations) {
65	    try {
66	      await db.execute(sql);
67	    } catch {
68	      // Column already exists - ignore
69	    }
70	  }
71	
72	  await db.execute(`

74	      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,

## claude.ai Gamma
Gamma creates AI-powered presentations, documents, webpages, and social posts. Only include optional parameters when the user explicitly requests or clearly implies them. Gamma has intelligent defaults - when in doubt, omit. When uncertain about user preferences, ask rather than assume. IMPORTANT: The tools provided by this server can only create new content — they cannot edit or modify existing Gammas. Do not suggest that you can make changes, adjustments, or refinements to a generated Gamma. Instead, let the user know they can customize their Gamma directly in the Gamma editor.

7	  await initDb();

11	function rowToQuote(row: Record<string, unknown>): Quote {
12	  return {
13	    id: row.id as string,
14	    clientName: row.client_name as string,
15	    clientEmail: row.client_email as string,
16	    clientCompany: (row.client_company as string) || "",
17	    title: row.title as string,
18	    description: (row.description as string) || "",
19	    quoteType: (row.quote_type as Quote["quoteType"]) || "line-items",
20	    lineItems: JSON.parse((row.line_items as string) || "[]") as LineItem[],
21	    currency: row.currency as Quote["currency"],
22	    discount: (row.discount as number) || 0,
23	    notes: (row.notes as string) || "",
24	    status: row.status as Quote["status"],
25	    validUntil: (row.valid_until as string) || "",
26	    locale: row.locale as Quote["locale"],
27	    createdAt: row.created_at as string,
28	    updatedAt: row.updated_at as string,
29	    problem: (row.problem as string) || undefined,
30	    opportunity: (row.opportunity as string) || undefined,
31	    deliverables: row.deliverables
32	      ? (JSON.parse(row.deliverables as string) as Deliverable[])
33	      : undefined,
34	    timeline: (row.timeline as string) || undefined,
35	    fixedPrice: row.fixed_price != null ? (row.fixed_price as number) : undefined,
36	    listPrice: row.list_price != null ? (row.list_price as number) : undefined,
37	    preferentialNote: (row.preferential_note as string) || undefined,
38	    paymentTerms: (row.payment_terms as string) || undefined,
39	    nextSteps: row.next_steps
40	      ? (JSON.parse(row.next_steps as string) as string[])
41	      : undefined,
42	    scopeNote: (row.scope_note as string) || undefined,
43	  };
44	}

46	export async function getAllQuotes(): Promise<Quote[]> {
47	  const db = await ensureDb();
48	  const result = await db.execute(
49	    "SELECT * FROM quotes ORDER BY created_at DESC"
50	  );
51	  return result.rows.map((row) => rowToQuote(row as Record<string, unknown>));
52	}
53	
54	export async function getQuoteById(id: string): Promise<Quote | null> {
55	  const db = await ensureDb();
56	  const result = await db.execute({
57	    sql: "SELECT * FROM quotes WHERE id = ?",
58	    args: [id],
59	  });
60	  if (result.rows.length === 0) return null;
61	  return rowToQuote(result.rows[0] as Record<string, unknown>);
62	}
63	
64	export async function createQuote(data: QuoteFormData): Promise<Quote> {
65	  const db = await ensureDb();
66	  const id = nanoid(10);
67	  const now = new Date().toISOString();
68	
69	  await db.execute({
70	    sql: `INSERT INTO quotes (
71	            id, client_name, client_email, client_company, title, description,
72	            quote_type, line_items, currency, discount, notes, status, valid_until, locale,
73	            problem, opportunity, deliverables, timeline,
74	            fixed_price, list_price, preferential_note, payment_terms, next_steps, scope_note,
75	            created_at, updated_at
76	          ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
77	    args: [
78	      id,
79	      data.clientName,
80	      data.clientEmail,
81	      data.clientCompany,
82	      data.title,
83	      data.description,
84	      data.quoteType || "line-items",
85	      JSON.stringify(data.lineItems),
86	      data.currency,
87	      data.discount,
88	      data.notes,
89	      data.status,
90	      data.validUntil,
91	      data.locale,
92	      data.problem || null,
93	      data.opportunity || null,
94	      data.deliverables ? JSON.stringify(data.deliverables) : null,
95	      data.timeline || null,
96	      data.fixedPrice ?? null,
97	      data.listPrice ?? null,
98	      data.preferentialNote || null,
99	      data.paymentTerms || null,
100	      data.nextSteps ? JSON.stringify(data.nextSteps) : null,
101	      data.scopeNote || null,
102	      now,
103	      now,
104	    ],
105	  });
106	
107	  return (await getQuoteById(id))!;
108	}

110	export async function updateQuote(
111	  id: string,
112	  data: Partial<QuoteFormData>
113	): Promise<Quote | null> {
114	  const db = await ensureDb();
115	  const existing = await getQuoteById(id);
116	  if (!existing) return null;
117	
118	  const updated = { ...existing, ...data };
119	  const now = new Date().toISOString();
120

123	            client_name = ?, client_email = ?, client_company = ?,
124	            title = ?, description = ?, quote_type = ?, line_items = ?,
125	            currency = ?, discount = ?, notes = ?,
126	            status = ?, valid_until = ?, locale = ?,
127	            problem = ?, opportunity = ?, deliverables = ?, timeline = ?,
128	            fixed_price = ?, list_price = ?, preferential_note = ?,
129	            payment_terms = ?, next_steps = ?, scope_note = ?,
130	            updated_at = ?
131	          WHERE id = ?`,
132	    args: [

134	      updated.clientEmail,
135	      updated.clientCompany,
136	      updated.title,
137	      updated.description,
138	      updated.quoteType || "line-items",

148	      updated.deliverables ? JSON.stringify(updated.deliverables) : null,
149	      updated.timeline || null,
150	      updated.fixedPrice ?? null,
151	      updated.listPrice ?? null,
152	      updated.preferentialNote || null,
153	      updated.paymentTerms || null,
154	      updated.nextSteps ? JSON.stringify(updated.nextSteps) : null,
155	      updated.scopeNote || null,
156	      now,
157	      id,
158	    ],
159	  });
160	
161	  return getQuoteById(id);
162	}

- [ ] Client portal with login

content.text:
All tasks complete. Here's a summary of everything implemented:

---

**Phase 1 + 2 implemented in this session:**

1. **Email notifications for contact form** — Installed `resend`, created `src/shared/lib/email.ts` with `sendContactNotification()` and `sendQuoteToClient()`. Contact form action now sends a formatted email on every submission (non-blocking, won't break the form if email fails).

2. **Admin contacts dashboard** — New `/admin/contacts` route with a table showing all contact form submissions. Supports read/unread badges, mark as read/unread, and delete. Added `read` column migration to `contact_submissions` table. Nav link in admin header.

3. **Realistic case studies** — Replaced placeholder "FinTech Startup" / "Manufacturing Corp" content with three projects that reflect PRAGMA's actual services: AI knowledge assistant (RAG), automated invoicing pipeline, and client portal/quoting platform. Both EN and ES.

4. **Vercel Analytics + Speed Insights** — Installed both packages and added to root layout. Zero-config after deploy.

5. **Quote email notifications** — New "Send to Client" button on the quote table (both mobile and desktop). Emails the shareable quote link via Resend, auto-updates status to "sent".

6. **Quote search, filter, and pagination** — Updated `getAllQuotes()` to support search (client name/company/title), filter by status and quote type, and pagination (15 per page). New `QuoteFilters` and `Pagination` components.

**Env vars to set on Vercel:**
- `RESEND_API_KEY` — from Resend dashboard (free tier: 3k emails/month)
- `ADMIN_EMAIL` — where contact notifications go (defaults to hello@pragmatech.ai)
- `FROM_EMAIL` — sender address (defaults to Resend onboarding address)
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` — for correct sitemap/quote URLs

import bcrypt from "bcryptjs";

const SESSION_COOKIE = "pragma_admin_session";
const SESSION_MAX_AGE = 60 * 60 * 24; // 24 hours

export async function verifyPassword(password: string): Promise<boolean> {
  const hash = process.env.ADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH;
  if (!hash) return false;
  return bcrypt.compare(password, hash);
}

export async function createSession(): Promise<void> {
  const token = crypto.randomUUID();
  const cookieStore = await cookies();
  cookieStore.set(SESSION_COOKIE, token, {
    httpOnly: true,
    secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === "production",
    sameSite: "lax",
    maxAge: SESSION_MAX_AGE,
    path: "/",
  });
}

export async function isAuthenticated(): Promise<boolean> {
  const cookieStore = await cookies();
  const session = cookieStore.get(SESSION_COOKIE);
  return !!session?.value;
}

export async function destroySession(): Promise<void> {
  const cookieStore = await cookies();
  cookieStore.delete(SESSION_COOKIE);
}

/Users/santiagocoronado/PRAGMA/pragma-web/logs/post_tool_use.json:        "content": "# PRAGMA Web

Landing page + quote system for PRAGMA — AI & Technology Solutions.

Built with Next.js 16, Turso (libSQL), react-pdf, next-intl (EN/ES), Tailwind CSS v4.

---

## Local development

```bash
npm install
cp .env.sample .env.local   # fill in values (see Environment variables below)
npm run dev
```

Seed the dev database with sample quotes:

```bash
# Option 1 — HTTP endpoint (server must be running)
curl http://localhost:3000/api/dev/seed

# Option 2 — script
npm run db:seed
```

### Scripts

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `npm run dev` | Start dev server |
| `npm run build` | Production build |
| `npm run lint` | ESLint |
| `npm test` | Unit + integration tests (Vitest) |
| `npm run test:watch` | Vitest in watch mode |
| `npm run test:e2e` | Playwright E2E (starts dev server automatically) |
| `npm run db:seed` | Seed dev DB with sample quotes |
| `node scripts/hash-password.mjs <pw>` | Generate bcrypt hash for ADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH |

---

## Environment variables

Copy `.env.sample` to `.env.local` and fill in every value before running locally or deploying.

### Database — Turso

| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `DATABASE_URL` | Turso database URL — format: `libsql://your-db-name.turso.io` |
| `DATABASE_AUTH_TOKEN` | Turso auth token — generate with `turso db tokens create <db-name>` |

### Admin auth

| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `ADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH` | bcrypt hash of the admin password. **Never store the plain password.** Generate with: `node scripts/hash-password.mjs <your-password>` |

### App URL

| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` | Full public URL of the site, no trailing slash — e.g. `https://pragma.agency`. Used for shareable quote links, sitemap, and OG metadata. |

### E2E tests (optional)

| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `E2E_ADMIN_PASSWORD` | Plain-text admin password used by Playwright to log in during E2E tests. Only needed locally for running `npm run test:e2e`. |
| `PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL` | Override the base URL for E2E tests. Defaults to `http://localhost:3000`. |

---

## Deployment

### Prerequisites

- [Vercel account](https://vercel.com) (free tier is fine)
- [Turso account](https://turso.tech) (free tier: 500MB, plenty for this)
- [Turso CLI](https://docs.turso.tech/cli/installation): `brew install tursodatabase/tap/turso`

---

### Step 1 — Provision the Turso production database

Run these commands once. Copy the output values — you'll need them for Vercel.

```bash
# Log in
turso auth login

# Create the production database
turso db create pragma-web-prod

# Get the database URL (copy the value under "URL")
turso db show pragma-web-prod

# Create an auth token (copy the full token string)
turso db tokens create pragma-web-prod
```

The schema is applied automatically on first run — no migration step needed.

---

### Step 2 — Generate the admin password hash

```bash
node scripts/hash-password.mjs <your-chosen-password>
# Outputs something like: $2b$12$...
# Copy this — you'll paste it as ADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH in Vercel
```

---

### Step 3 — Deploy to Vercel

```bash
# Install Vercel CLI if you don't have it
npm i -g vercel

# From the project root — follow the prompts
# Link to your GitHub repo when asked, select the SantiagoCoronado/pragma-web repo
vercel

# Or deploy directly to production
vercel --prod
```

Alternatively: go to [vercel.com/new](https://vercel.com/new), import from GitHub, and follow the UI.

---

### Step 4 — Set environment variables on Vercel

Run each command and paste the value when prompted:

```bash
vercel env add DATABASE_URL production
# paste: libsql://pragma-web-prod.turso.io  (from Step 1)

vercel env add DATABASE_AUTH_TOKEN production
# paste: your Turso auth token  (from Step 1)

vercel env add ADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH production
# paste: $2b$12$...  (from Step 2)

vercel env add NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL production
# paste: https://pragma.agency  (your actual domain)
```

Then trigger a fresh deployment to pick up the new vars:

```bash
vercel --prod
```

---

### Step 5 — Connect your domain (optional)

In the Vercel dashboard → your project → Settings → Domains, add `pragma.agency` and follow the DNS instructions.

---

### Step 6 — Production smoke test checklist

After deploying, verify manually:

- [ ] `https://pragma.agency/en` — landing page loads, all sections visible
- [ ] `https://pragma.agency/es` — Spanish version loads
- [ ] `https://pragma.agency/sitemap.xml` — returns XML with `/en` and `/es`
- [ ] `https://pragma.agency/robots.txt` — disallows `/en/admin/`, `/en/quote/`
- [ ] `https://pragma.agency/en/admin` — login form appears
- [ ] Admin login works with your password
- [ ] Create a new quote, save as draft
- [ ] Copy shareable link → open in a new incognito tab → quote renders
- [ ] Download PDF → file opens, looks correct
- [ ] `https://pragma.agency/en/admin` shows noindex in page source

---

## Architecture

```
src/
├── app/
│   ├── [locale]/          # EN + ES routes (landing, admin, quote viewer)
│   ├── api/
│   │   ├── quote/[id]/pdf/    # PDF generation endpoint
│   │   └── dev/seed/          # Dev-only DB seeder (disabled in production)
│   ├── sitemap.ts
│   └── robots.ts
├── features/
│   ├── quotes/            # Quote form, viewer, PDF, server actions, DB queries
│   ├── landing/           # Hero, Services, About, Case Studies, Testimonials, Contact
│   └── auth/              # Login form, server action, session management
└── shared/
    ├── components/
    │   ├── layout/        # Navbar, Footer, LanguageToggle
    │   ├── ui/            # Button, Card, Badge, Input, Toaster, AnimateIn, ...
    │   └── seo/           # JsonLd
    ├── hooks/             # useMediaQuery, useScrollPosition
    └── lib/               # db.ts, rate-limit.ts
```

### Quote types

| Type | Description |
|---|---|
| `line-items` | Itemized quote with qty × price table, subtotal, discount, total |
| `blueprint` | Narrative proposal — problem, opportunity, deliverables, timeline, fixed price |

Both types generate a branded PDF with the PRAGMA logo and footer.

### Auth

Single-admin password auth using bcrypt. Session stored in an `httpOnly` cookie (`SameSite: Lax`, 24h). Rate-limited to 5 login attempts per IP per 15 minutes. CSRF handled by Next.js Server Actions (Origin header validation).

content.text: Now hit `http://localhost:3000/api/debug-auth` and share the output.

The hash length is 44 — a valid bcrypt hash is 60 chars. Next.js's dotenv parser is expanding the `$` signs in the hash (treating `$2b`, `$12`, etc. as shell variables). The fix is to single-quote the value in `.env.local`.

Update the script to wrap the hash in single quotes:

input.old_string: content += `\nADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH=${hash}\n`;
input.new_string: content += `\nADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH='${hash}'\n`;
